codeReview.summary: The TEN-09 prove-first packet is narrowly scoped and evidence-backed. It adds a validation-first bundle plus runtime/OpenAPI/test artifacts that demonstrate the questioned control-plane and portal surfaces already exist, that their nested payload families match controller/DTO code and published OpenAPI, and that the remaining drift is limited to timestamp serialization on the live runtime. The handoff shows targeted proof commands, saved logs, and compose-backed route captures, and the commit stays confined to the Lane 01 executable-spec packet artifacts.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The scrutiny-validator procedure needs an explicit documented fallback for per-feature reviews when `scrutiny-feature-reviewer` Task fan-out is unavailable because of model-alias resolution errors.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: In this scrutiny session, all four `scrutiny-feature-reviewer` Task launches for Lane 01 failed immediately with `Invalid model: custom:CLIProxyAPI-5.4-high`, so the validator had to review handoff JSON, transcript skeletons, and commit diffs directly and author the review reports in-session.

summary: PASS. The prove-first bundle correctly classifies TEN-09 as a narrow backend timestamp-parity defect and provides the route-by-route evidence needed before Lane 01 implementation widened.
